title: Trigonometry Basics
description: Label the sides of a right-angled triangle, apply Pythagoras' theorem, and use SOHCAHTOA and inverse trig functions to find missing sides and angles.
objectives: Label the hypotenuse, opposite and adjacent sides of a right-angled triangle relative to a given angle
Use Pythagoras' theorem (a^2 + b^2 = c^2) to find a missing side when no angle is involved
State and apply SOHCAHTOA to find a missing side, given one angle and one side
Use the inverse trig functions sin^-1, cos^-1 and tan^-1 to find a missing angle, given two sides
Decide which tool (Pythagoras or SOHCAHTOA) fits a given right-angled-triangle problem

lessons.title: Pythagoras' theorem and labelling triangle sides
